question.text: Which entity has been recognized by SEBI for the administration and supervision of investment advisers?

answer.text: AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India)
BSE Administration & Supervision Ltd (BASL), a subsidiary of BSE
SEBI directly administers all investment advisers without a recognized body

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** BSE Administration & Supervision Ltd (BASL), a subsidiary of BSE, has been recognized by SEBI for the administration and supervision of investment advisers. The stock exchange seeking recognition must meet specific criteria: minimum 15 years of existence, net worth of Rs. 200 crore, nationwide terminals, and Investor Service Centres (ISCs) in 20+ cities.
**The Trap:** AMFI administers mutual fund distributors, not investment advisers. NSE does not have a recognized subsidiary for this purpose. And SEBI does not directly administer IAs — it works through a recognized body.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The role of BASL is specific to NISM Series X-A. In Series V-A (MF Distributors), AMFI plays the corresponding administrative role for MF distributors.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain your BASL membership and comply with all BASL reporting requirements. BASL conducts periodic compliance checks on behalf of SEBI — treat BASL correspondence with the same seriousness as direct SEBI communications.
